What's going on everyone? How to handle refunds. Everyone's favorite topic. So there are some best practices here and to make sure that you handle things well with everyone I like to give some best practice. So first off don't live by contracts. Lives don't know when to sign anything and everything is based on how amazingly you serve them. Alright so this is just kind of something to live by and I mean this genuinely like if you feel like today. And so you have to prove to them that it's worth. Don't respect contracts if you feel like you're not holding up your side of the bargain. They need to deliver great service and you need to deliver it all the way through and once they're members as well. So don't live by contracts. Lives don't know when to sign one and fulfill from there. Second if someone hits the goal and they don't and they want their money back give it to them. Simple as that. After them I would ask one question before that just saying like the satisfaction guarantee is based on whether you feel like we delivered poor service. It was not based on whether you hit the goal or not because that's something that we can't control. Do you feel like we give you bad service? Do you feel like the trainers give you bad service? Which trainer do you feel like give you poor service? If they're like oh okay well then so you basically want to walk them through that conversation and see if there's anything else you can do for them to make them satisfied. But at the end of the day if someone wants their money back this isn't for me. I want my money back. I would know for the period of time that you were here which would be at a normal rate would be $100 or whatever for the two weeks or three weeks whatever it is that you were here. A lot of times they're like yeah that sounds fair then you can refund the remainder. Easy. You have to accept that people are crazy and it's a numbers game. So if you get 100 sales you're going to have some people back out. Period. You're going to have some people be crazy people. If you thought you could take a random sampling of 100 people in the United States what percentage do you think are crazy? I would say at least 5 or 10 percent. So it's not crazy for crazy people to do crazy things. It's crazy for same people to expect crazy people to do same things. So don't be crazy. Expect crazy people to do crazy things. So if someone two months after their challenge is over or they can't use discernment or try to use reason. But at the end of the day with all of these situations if someone really wants their money back we give them their money back. So I want you to adopt that. You always want to be reasonable and see if there are things that you can do. Why don't we just get you started again. Asking the main questions like have you lost weight yet? Have you started doing anything to be healthy? No. How about this? Let me make it up to you. We'll forget about the last one. The last six weeks even though you did two weeks of it. We'll just start you over again free of charge on me. I want to help you out. And you should mean that. Which you should. And sometimes those people will come back. Like insert crazy situation that isn't your fault. I'm pregnant. My car crashed. Like there was a joke that was in one of the threads that we had in the gym owner's group. Which was like definitely don't sign up for a gym because if you do that the likelihood that someone in your immediate family gets cancer or you're in a car accident or you get pregnant you're quadruples. And that's because people lie and they just use excuses. And you can try and actually find the logic of their excuses which is usually faulty. Or give their money back and go sell someone else and not worry about it. So attention to money. If you're consuming any of my content you know that I care a lot about it. The amount of time and attention it takes you and annoyance of having these people on your lines. On your attention lines of communication. It's just not worth dealing with them. It makes you fall out of love with your existing clients. So my experience if someone wanted their money back I tried to find amicable solutions that I would be able to serve them without doing that and if they still thought that way I gave them their money back. Alright, real solution and if they don't give them their money back. And when you do that this makes the offer on the front end so irresistible is that we offer money back. Mind them what part of the service like who and usually they won't want to name. Is it actually the service or is it the service that you're upset that your body didn't respond in a certain way. Well I'm kind of upset about that. Okay, well and then you got to reinforce like okay so you made progress you didn't hit 25 but you hit 13 that's still amazing it's more than you did in the past. So you have to sell them sometimes and I can't tell you the amount of times that someone can tell me what's going on in their life. I'm like cool Rosie I totally hear you like do you still want to lose weight? Oh yeah well then let's do it. Handle it that way. You show this to your operations manager to your team actually try and serve them. If all else fails then my own man's give them the refund don't try and hold it hostage it's not the right way to do business. Alright lots of love and I'll see you soon.
